def: s.f. (see gorā), A fair-complexioned woman; — a beauty, belle; a mistress.
more: n. f. S. ग्रुह take. Profit; gain. goṛī k. , v. a. 1. To earn; gain; make. Āj kyā goṛī kar lāë? 2. To bag; pocket. goṛī jamānā , v. n. See ṭippas jamānā. goṛī hāth se jānā , v. n. To lose one's prey. Bhaī! kyā goṛī hāth se gaī hai!

def: s.f. Taking away, carrying off; stealing; theft; — profit, gain, earnings; — plunder, booty, prey
more: s. f. Taking away. گوڙي کرنا goṛī karnā, To take away, to steal, to walk off with.
subs: v.t. To take away, carry off; to earn, make, gain; to walk off with, to steal
To lose (one's) plunder, or booty, or prey.

def: s.m. = P گورخر gor-ḵẖar, s.m. See s.v. gor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گورِسْتَان (gōristān). By surface analysis, گور (gor) + ـسْتَان (-stān).

def: s.m. lit. 'Cow-keeper'; a Gorkhā, or inhabitant of Nepāl; (fig.) a short man (the Gorkhās being short).
more: n. m. 1. A Gorkhā . 2. A short man.
wety: Borrowed from Nepali गोर्खा (gorkhā, “(inhabitant of) Gorkha District”) named for गोरख (gorakh, “Gorakhnath”), from Sanskrit गोरक्ष (gorakṣa, “cowherd”).

def: s.m. = S گورستان gor-istān, s.m. See s.v. gor.
more: s. m. A wild ass, onager.

def: v.t. = goṛhnā, and godnā, qq.v.; — s.m. = godnā, q.v. v.t. To scrape, scratch; to dig, to excavate; to hollow out, scoop out.
more: v. a. To dig, to scrape.

s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)

ety: S. गो+रूपः or रूपा and रूपकः
def: s.m. The time of bringing home the cattle; sunset, twilight, evening.
more: s. m. An ox, a cow. گورواڐهکان gorūʼā-ḍhukān, s. m. Twilight (the time of bringing home the cows). گورواري بيرا gorūʼārī berā, s. f. Evening.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गौरव (gaurava, “cumbrousness, importance”); see गौर (gaura, “shining, brilliant”) for more.

ety: S. गो+रूपः or रूपा and रूपकः
def: s.m. The time of bringing home the cattle; sunset, twilight, evening.
s.m. A measure of distance equal to two kos (or as far as the lowing of a cow may be heard).
s.f. The time for the cattle to be brought home; twilight, evening.
s.m. Cow-dung; dried cow-dung (syn. uplā).
( गोस्वामी comp. of गो Cow, kine, and स्वामी Master) s. m. 1. The deity. 2. A saint, a holy person. 3. The descendants of the disciples of Chaitanya of Nadiyā.
s.m. = gusāṅʼī or gusāʼīṅ, and go-svāmī, qq.v. (the last s.v. go).
s. m. The deity.

def: s.f. A sheep; a goat.
more: s. f. A sheep, a goat.

ety: old P. gausa; Pārsī and Pehl. gosh; Zend gaosha, fr. gush, 'to hear'; cf. S. घोष, rt. घुष्
def: s.m. The ear (syn. kān)
more: n. m. The ear (کان 1). gosh-band , n. m. A bandage over the ears. gosh-zad h. , v. n. To be heard. gosh-guzār k. , v. a. To report; inform. gosh-guzārī , n. f. Information; notice. gosh-mālī , n. f. 1. Pulling the ears; chastisement. 2. Rebuke; reproof. gosh-wārah , n. m. 1. See طره (3). 2. E. Boring the ears (girls). 3. The abstract of an account.

s. m. The ear. گوشبند gosh-band, A bandage over the ears. گوش پيچ gosh-pech, An ornament worn in the turband. گوش دار gosh-dār, adj. Taking care, keeper, protector. گوش داري gosh-dār-ī, s. f. Care, attention, protection. گوش زد هونا gosh-zad honā, To be listened to, to be heard. گوش گذار gosh-guẕār, Heard, reported. گوش گذار کرنا gosh-guẕār karnā, To make pass in the ear, to hear or to make to hear. گوش گذارهونا gosh-guẕār honā, To be heard. گوشمال gosh-māl, Pulling (or rubbing, or boxing) the ears. گوشمالي goshmāl-ī, s. f. Rebuke, chastisement.
s. (dakh.) for گوشت gosht, q. v.
subs: s.m. A bandage over the ears
s.m. A cap covering the ears against cold; — a shawl twisted round the head and ears (and worn as a turban); — an ornament worn in the turban
adj. & s.m. Taking care of; — keeper, preserver, protector, guardian
s.f. Preservation, protection; care, attention; — gosh-zad honā, v.n. 'To strike the ear,' to be heard, to be listened to
part. adj. 'Passed into the ear,' heard; reported
v.t. 'To cause to pass into the ear' (of, -ke), to cause to hear, to inform; to report (to); to impart, to communicate
or gosh-mālī, s.f. Rubbing or twisting the ears (by way of punishment); chastisement; — rebuke, reproof.

subs: s.m. A gleaner; (fig.) recipient of a benefaction

def: s.m. Angle; corner; nook, closet; cell; — retirement; privacy; seclusion; — side; end; quarter; — handle (of a vessel, &c.); — horn (of a bow)
more: n. m. 1. See گونه 2. The horn (of a bow). 3. A hermit's cell; retirement; privacy. gosha-i-junūb o mashriq ; S. अग्निकोण South-East. gosha-i-junūb o magrib ; S. नैऋतिकोण South-West. gosha-i-chashm , The corner of the eye ( koyah , 1). goshe-dār , adj. Cornered; angular. gosha-i-shimāl o mashriq ; S. ईशानकोण North-East. gosha-i-shimāl o magrib ; S. वायव्यरोण North-West. goshe-kushādah , Open ends. goshe meṅ , adv. In privacy or retirement. gosha-nashīṅ , n. m. A hermit; recluse. gosha-nashīṅ , adj. Retired; secluded. gosha-nashīnī , n. f. Seclusion; a hermit's life. kamān ke goshe meṅ pakaṛnā ; Obs. lit . to catch with the bow without shooting. To seize with facility.

s. m. 1. A corner, an angle, a closet, cell, retirement, privacy. 2. Side. 3. Horn (of a bow). گوشي دار goshe-dār, adj. Angular. گوشه گزين gosha-guzīn, Fond of retirement. گوشه گير gosha-gīr, 1. Retired. 2. (substant.) A hermit. گوشه گيري gosha-gīr-ī, s. f. Retirement, life of a hermit. گوشه نشين gosha-nishīn, Retired (sitting in a corner), a hermit, solitary. گوشه نشيني gosha-nishīn-ī, s. f. Retirement, the life of a hermit. کمان کي گوشي مين پکڙنا kamān ke goshe meṅ pakaṛnā, (lit. to catch with the bow without shooting) To seize with facility.
subs: vulg. goshe-kushāda, adj. Having the end or ends open

ety: Pehl. gosht; Zend geus, gen. of gāo+tanu; S. gos, gen. of go+tanu
def: s.m. Meat; — flesh; — internal matter or substance; pulp (of fruit)
more: n. m. Meat; flesh. Gosht khāë gosht baṛhe; ghī khāë bal hoë, Sāg khāë ojh baṛhe; bal kahāṅ se hoë? Eat flesh you flesh put on, on butter strong you grow, On potherbs you 've a paunch, but strength they dont bestow. gosht-khuār, gosht-khār , adj. Carnivorous; flesh-eating. gosht kā lothṛā , 1. A lump of flesh. 2. A fat man. 3. Membrum virile. Khudā ne tumhāre ek gosht kā lothṛā lagā-ke nāhaq mard banā diyā . Wom. (Said to an impotent). be-haḍḍī kā gosht , n. m. 1. Mere flesh without bones. 2. ( Slang ) The penis . 3. ( Slang ) A woman's breasts.
s. m. Meat, flesh. گوشت ناخن هونا gosht-i-nāḵẖun honā (to be the flesh of the nails) Denotes intimate friendship.

more: adj. Fleshy (an epithet chiefly applied to the tongue; as, in this phrase: زبان گوشتين است تيغ آهنين zabān goshtīn ast teg̠ẖ āhanīn, The tongue, though of flesh, is yet a sword of steel).

more: s. m. 1. An earring. 2. An embroidered cloth worn as an ornament over the sides of a turband. 3. The abstract of an account. 4. Boring the ears (of female children).

ety: prob. for A. g̠aut̤, inf. n. of غوط 'to enter, to sink' (into
def: s.m. Sinking (into), diving; a plunge (into water), a dive; — consideration, deep meditation; — a swoon, fainting-fit
more: n. m. 1. Deep meditation ( gaur , 1). 2. A faint; swoon. Bachcha got meṅ paṛā hai. got mārnā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To dive; disappear. 2. To think deeply; go to the root of a matter. 3. To give oneself up to meditation. got meṅ ānā , v. n. To faint; swoon.
(from غوطه q. v.) s. m. Consideration. غوط مارنا g̠ẖot̤ mārnā, To disappear. غوط مين انا يا جا نا g̠ẖot̤ meṅ ānā or jānā, To faint.
subs: or g̠ot̤ mārnā (-meṅ), To plunge or dive (into); to disappear; to think deeply (on), be engaged in meditation (on), be absorbed (in)

ety: S. गो+त्रं, and त्रकं, rt. त्रै
def: s.m. Family, race, parentage, lineage, kith and kin, family stock; family name; a caste (collectively); subdivision of a tribe or caste; genus; class or species.
more: n. m.S. गौत्र 1. Lineage (اصل 5). 2. A family; a tribe. 1. Nāt kā, na got kā, bāṅṭā māṅge poth kā . Rus. Prov. Nor relative nor of the race, a share to ask he has the face. 2. Tamkhere sūṅ āp tamākhā seroṅ din din lāūṅgā, Gotī, mītbiṭhā-kar sagre hūkā dinon piyāūṅgā ! Rus. A seer of tobacco I'll fetch from the shop every day, With my brethren and friends gathered round me we'll all smoke away. 3. A sub-division of any common tribe or caste. Sāṛhe sattarah got ki lāj! Bards. The honor of seventeen and a half races rests with you! gotī, gotī-bhāī , n. m. One of the same family or stock; a kinsman. Jo moë dego chunnī bhussī, vāke rahūṅgo apnī khussī, Jo moë dego karrā jotā, vāko maryo kunbā gotā! Rus. The bullock's appeal. Who feeds me well with him I will remain, Who works me hard may all his kith be slain!
n. f. S. गुटिका 1. The border or edging of a garment. 2. A piece in chausar and pachchīsī , q. v. 3. A company. 4. ( Māṛ .) Feast; entertainment. 5. A village. goṭ bastī , n. f. A village site.
s. m. Parentage, lineage, pedigree, stock of a family.

def: s.m. Mustard-seed(?).

s.m. Edging; gold or silver lace (rather narrow); a narrow fillet of brocade; a preparation of coriander seeds, cocoa-nut, cardamoms, and coffee, &c. (in this sense perhaps from Prk. goṭṭha; S. goshṭha)
more: s. m. (gold or silver) Lace, or edging (rather narrow).
subs: s.f. Edging of gold or silver lace
s.m. A maker (or a seller) of edging, lace, braid, &c.

ety: prob. for A. غوطة g̠aut̤at, n. of un. fr. g̠aut̤; see g̠ot̤
def: s.m. Diving, plunging (into); dipping, immersion; a plunge (into water), a dive, a dip
more: n. m. A dip; dive; ducking. gotah denā , v. a. 1. To dip; plunge or duck under water. 2. To baptize. 3. To deceive ( dhokā d .). gotah khānā , v. n. 1. ( gota lagānā ) To be dipped; to dive. Chakrātī, taṛapti, phaṛphaṛātī, Gairat meṅ chalī woh gote khātī . The paper kite. Sohan Lāl. Circling, quivering, fluttering down Reeling with shame to earth anon. 2. To go astray; lose one's way. 3. To be tricked, cheated ( jul meṅ ānā ). Tū to chanchal, baṛī chatar hī, yāṅ kyoṅ gotā khāyā? Jāne nāhīṅ sāt hāth kī gharoṅ chhor-ke āyā! Jo tujh ko udmād lagā hā raṅḍvā koī na pāyā? Mere pīyā ke chhal meṅ ā gaī, yo haigā chhal- hāyā: Nigoḍī! nyānat-mārī? pīyā ne tohe bīsārī! Quarrel of co-wives. Play. So clever and so skittish! yet to be so grossly choused! What! knew you not a strapping wife was here already housed? When you became so very hot a widower could n't you get? Caught in my husband's shares! …

(from Pers. غوته) s. m. 1. Dipping, diving, immersion. 2. A dip, a dive, a plunge. غوطه باز g̠ẖot̤a-bāz, A diver. غوطه خور g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖor, A diver, any thing that dips, or is dipped. غوطه خورد g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖụrd, or غوطه خورده g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖụrda, Dipped, plunged. غوطه خوري g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. Dipping, being dipt. غوطه دينا g̠ẖot̤a-denā, To dip, to plunge under water. غوطه کهـانا g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖānā, 1. To be dipt. 2. To dive. 3. To be deceived. 4. To travel in a miry road. 5. To stray, to wander, to lose oneʼs way. غوطه مارنا g̠ẖot̤a mārnā, To plunge. dive, dip. غوطي مين جانا g̠ẖot̤e meṅ jānā, To faint.
subs: vulg. g̠ot̤e-ḵẖor, s.m. A diver; — anything that dips or is dipped; a sort of fire-work (so called because, being lighted, it is thrown into water, when it dives, and every now and then shows above water)
or g̠ot̤a-ḵẖẉurda, part. adj. Plunged (into), dipped, immersed
vulg. g̠ot̤e-ḵẖorī, Dipping, diving; being dipped
(-ko), To plunge (into water), to dip, to duck; to baptize; — to deceive
v.n. To be dipped, to suffer immersion; to dive; — to be deceived; to travel in a miry or treacherous road; — to stray, wander, lose (one's) way
or g̠ot̤a mārnā (-meṅ), To plunge into, to dive; — to think deeply (on), enter deeply (into), be absorbed (in)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غوطه, from Arabic غَوْطَة (ḡawṭa).

def: s.m. corr. of غوطه g̠ota, q.v.
s.m. Name of a celebrated Rishi, author of several hymns of the Vedas; — name of a sage, the founder of the Nyāya philosophy
more: n. prop. The name of a muni, or saint, famous in Hindū mythology. He was the author of the Nyāya, or logick, and his doctrines corresponded with those of Aristotle. Gautama and his wife Ahalyā lived in a retired forest, where Indra, happening to pass, was struck with the beauty of the latter; and, assuming the appearance of Gautama, deceived and slept with his wife: which being discovered, Gautama cursed them both. To Indra he said, may your testicles fall to the ground; which accordingly took place: but Brahmā advised him to restore them with those of a ram, cut out for that purpose; hence, Indra is called मेषांडकोष mesh̤ānḍakosh̤a (ram-testicled). He wished that Ahalyā might be turned into stone; in which state she remained till Rūmachandra (son of Dasaratha ) passing that way with his brother Lachh̤maṇ, relieved her and restored her to her original form: and, being thus purified by Rā…
subs: (˚ma+ar˚), s.m. The forest in Central India in which Gotam Rishi practised his austerities
s.f. An epithet of Ahalyā the wife of Gotam Rishi (she was turned into stone by her husband for her adultery with Indra).

def: s.m. A game in which swimmers dip or duck one another under water; hide-and-seek under water; — making a kite swoop in flying it.

A shark, crocodile; alligator; any large marine animal; — taking, seizing, seizure; receiving, accepting, acceptance; — a robber; — a morbid affection; a fit; disease; — an undertaking, enterprise.
more: n. m. A crocodile. Banye kā mūṅh girāh, aur peṭ mom . Prov. The Banya has the jaws of an alligator, but a stomach of wax. (He pinches himself to hoard money).
s. m. An alligator, a shark.

def: s.m. The power that seizes and obscures the sun and moon, especially Rāhu or the ascending node; — seizure of the sun or moon, an eclipse ( = grahaṇ); — a planet (as seizing or influencing the destinies of men in a supernatural manner: the Hindū mythology recognizes nine planets, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rāhu, Ketu, Sun, and Moon; hence) a symbol for the number 9; — name of certain evil spirits who seize or exercise a bad influence on the body and mind of man (causing insanity, &c.)
subs: (˚ha+ādh˚),s.m. The polar star (as the fixed centre of the planets)
(˚ha+ām˚), s.m. Epilepsy, convulsions, demoniacal possession (which is supposed to cause the complaint)
(-par), To come under the influence of an evil star; to be possessed by a demon or evil spirit
s.m. The chief of the planets, the sun
s.f. Worship of the sun and other planets
s.f. The pain or oppression caused by a planet; the influence of an unpropitious planet
vulg. grah-dasā, or grah-diśā, s.f. The aspect of the planets; the influence of an evil star; evil days; planetary influence (good or evil)
s.m. The chief of the planets; — the sun; the moon; the planet Jupiter
s.m. The invoking the presence of the nine planets

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Taking, receiving, accepting; apprehending, perceiving; seizing, catching; holding; robbing; choosing; gaining; gathering; enclosing; charming; — one who takes or receives; taker, receiver; seizer; buyer, purchaser; — a constable, a bailiff; a hawk.

n. m. 1. Seizing; taking; acceptance. 2. An eclipse. 1. Grahan bairī chāṅd kā, aur dīpak bairī paun, Jī kā bairī kāl hai, āvat roke kaun? The moon's foe is the eclipse, as wind of light, Life's foe is Death, who can resist its might? 2. Girahn meṅ peṭ-vālī ko bāhar nikalnā achchhā nahīṅ . It is not good for a pregnant woman to come out of her house during an eclipse. grahn paṛnā , v. n. To be eclipsed. grahn k. , v. a. To admit; accept; agree to. thoṛā grahn , A partial eclipse.

s.m. Seizing, holding, taking, catching, clasping; seizure, arrest, apprehension; receiving, accepting, acceptance; seizure of the sun or moon (by Rāhu), an eclipse; — deriving, acquirement, acquisition; buying; putting on; protection; undergoing; study; perceiving, perception, understanding, comprehension; pronouncing
more: s. m. 1. An eclipse. 2. Seizing, taking.
subs: An eclipse to take place; to be eclipsed
s.m. Bathing (for religious purposes) at the time of an eclipse
v.t. To take, seize, clasp; to receive, accept; to admit, agree to, assent to; to derive; to perceive, comprehend; to smell, &c. (see grahaṇ).

def: s.f. The state or condition of being seized, or eclipsed, &c. (see grahaṇ).
s.f. An imaginary organ supposed to lie between the stomach and the intestines; — diarrhœa; dysentery.

n. m. S. गृहस्थ See dunyā-dārī , 1—4. 1. Domestic or worldly affairs. Jog āsān, grahast kaṭhin . Prov. Easy a holy friar to be, hard house affairs and husbandry. 2. Husbandry; agriculture. 3. Housekeeping. grahast'an, girastan , n. f. A married woman; a housewife.
s. m. Invoking the presence of the nine planets.
n. m. गृहस्थ; Pālī, gahatṭho . See dunyā-dār . 1. One who lives and marries; a householder. 2. A husbandman. grahastī kā aṭālā , Household goods or furniture.

Living in a village or in villages; — inhabitant of a village, a villager

def: n. f. 1. ( gāṅṭh ) A knot. 2. A book; code ( pushtak ). 3. A religious book. 4. ( Ādī granth, granth Sāhab ). The sacred book of the Sikhs , a large collection of short moral couplets by Nānak and others in Panjābī and Hindī.
s.m. A character in which Sanskrit is written and printed in Southern India (where the Devanāgarī character is unknown).
s.m. Stringing, tying or connecting together (either as a chaplet or a book), arranging, composing; — (in Braj) nom. pl. of granth, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Bookish, well-read, learned; relating or belonging to a book; — one who reads books; a writer of books; one who possesses many books.

s. m. An author, book-maker, writer, compiler.

def: s.f. The neck; nape of the neck; — the tendon of the Trapezium muscle.

ety: for gūh; S. गूथं, rt. गू
def: s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
more: Sant. ī : n. f. S. गूथ Excrement ( bhishṭā ). 1. Gū dar gū, murgī kā gū . Prov. 2. Gū kā ṭokrā sir par uṭhātā hūṅ . Prov. A basket of filth I bear on my head. (Said of mean work or disgrace). gū uchhālnā , v. n. To bring disgrace upon oneself. gū se ghināonā k .; Bhoj. gūho seṅ ghīnāvan kail , v. a. To render one more despicable than filth. gū kā kīṛā , n. 1. A maggot formed in human excrements. Gū kā kīṛā gū hī meṅ khush rahtā hai . Prov. The maggot born of filth filth enjoys. gū k. , v. a. To dirty; spoil. gū khānā , v. n. 1. To eat filth. 2. To do a shameful act. 3. To tell a lie. gū mūt k. , Wom. v. a. To be a nurse to a child. gū mūṅh meṅ denā , v. a. To shame; heap reproaches on. gū h. , v. n. To be dirtied, spolit. guhāṅj'nī , n. f. A sty ( anjanhārī , 1).
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.m. A ball; anything round.

ety: v.n. fr. غبر 'to pass, or pass away,' &c.
def: s.m. Dust; clouds of dust; a dust-storm; vapour, fog, mist, mistiness; impurity, foulness; (met.) vexation, soreness, ill-feeling, rancour, spite; affliction, grief; perplexity; — the smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting ( = ḵẖat̤-ě-g̠ubār)
more: n. m. غبر was dust colored. 1. Clouds of dust; a dust storm. 2. Fog; mist. 3. Foulness; impurity. 4. Hatred ( bair , 1). 5. Soreness; bad blood ( chashmak ). 6. Affliction; grief. 7. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting. gubār-ālūdah . Covered with dust; dusty; begrimed. gubār nikālnā , v. a. 1. To give vent to passion. 2. To take revenge ( bair nikālnā ).

s. m. 1. Dust. 2. Vapour. 3. Clouds of dust, impurity, foulness. 4. ( met. ) Vexation, affliction, perplexity. 5. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting. غبار آلود g̠ẖubār-ālūd or غبارآلوده g̠ẖubār-ālūda, Covered or polluted with dust, dusty. غبار خاطر g̠ẖubār-i-ḵẖāt̤ir, Affliction, trouble of mind.
subs: (apnā), To give vent to (one's) ill-feeling, &c.; to take revenge.

(fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave

more: n. m. 1. Old cotton, esp. what is taken out of an old quilt. 2. ( gūdaṛ gādaṛ ) Old tattered clothes, or rags. gūdaṛ gāṅṭhnā , v. a. To stitch together tattered clothes. gūdaṛ meṅ giṅdauṛā, gūdaṛ kā lāl . A jewel in rags. Garīb ho gaë to kyā, ab bhī gūdaṛ ke lāl haiṅ .

def: s.m. A quilt; — old cotton (esp. what is taken out of an old quilt); a bundle of old clothes, or of rags and tatters; any old tattered clothes; — a kind of very soft silk stuff
more: s. m. 1. A quilt. 2. A bundle of old clothes. 3. Any old tattered clothes. 4. A kind of silk stuff. گودڙ خيل gūdaṛ-ḵẖail, adj. (applied only to females) Foolish, unmannerly, rude. گودڙ سينا gūdaṛ sīnā, To stitch together. گودڙ کالال gūdaṛ kā lāl or گودڙ مين گندوڙا نکلنا gūdaṛ meṅ gandauṛā nikalnā, Good proceeding from evil.
subs: adj. f. Foolish, silly; unmannerly, rude
or gūdaṛ-meṅ giṅdaurā, s.m. A jewel in rags; — good out of evil
s.m. Old tattered clothes; rags and tatters
or gūdaṛ sīnā, To stitch tatters together.

ety: i.q. gudṛī, by substitution of S. इकः for इका
def: adj. & s.m. Clothed in patched garments or in rags; — one so clothed, a tatterdemalion; a slovenly or shabby fellow; — one who sells or hires out quilts, tents, &c.
more: 1. adj. Clothed in patched garments or in rags; a tatterdemalion. 2. One who lets quilts for hire. گدڙياپير gudaṛiyā-pīr, A tree near a town or village on which people tie up rags in the manner of votive tablets, which they believe to be efficacious for the accomplishment of their wishes.
subs: s.m. A tree near a town or village (generally one which is supposed to be haunted) on which people tie up rags in the manner of votive tablets, which they believe to be efficacious for the accomplishment of their wishes.

def: n. 1. One clothed in patched garments or in rags; a tatterdemalion. 2. One who sells or hires out quilts, tents, etc. 3. A slovenly or shabby fellow.
more: n. m. S. गडुरिया. H. gādaṛ a sheep. A shepherd by caste or occupation. Ahīr dekhe gaḍaryā mastānā . Prov. The shepherd got drunk seeing the cowkeeper drunk. (The ahīr's is a higher caste). gaḍaryā-purān , n. m. A shepherd's tale. 2. Rustic lore. gaḍaryoṅ kā gīt , n. m. 1. A shepherd's song. 2. A pastoral ( ālā ).

def: v.n. corr. of guẕarnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to pass; to bring to pass; to pass (life, or time, &c.); to pass by; — to pass or lay before; to exhibit; to state.

part. adj. Melted, dissolved; — mild, gentle, affable; tender, soft; plump, stout; thick; — (in comp.) melting, dissolving; consuming; exterminating; — melter; refiner (e.g. dil-gudāz, 'heart-melting'; — z̤ulm-gudāz, 'exterminator of oppression'; — pl. gudāzān, 'melters; refiners'); — s.m. Melting; liquefaction; consumption; anguish.
more: adj. 1. Thick; plump; stout. 2. Melting; tender; soft. Dil gudāz hai .
(from گداختن) 1. adj. Melted, dissolved, mild, gentle, affable. 2. (in compos.) part. act. Melting, melter, dissolver. 3. sub. Liquefaction, melting, dissolution.

ety: imperf. part. of gudāḵẖtan, and = gudāz, q.v.+ān = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: part. adj. Melting, dissolving; refining (gold).
more: part. act. (of گداختن) Melting, melter, dissolving.

ety: prob. S. गुडकः; or akin to guṭṭā, q.v.
def: s.m. A doll, a puppet; an effigy (the figure being that of a man; cf. guṛiyā); — an object of scorn, a monument of infamy
subs: (-kā), To make an effigy (of); — to set up as an object of scorn.
ex:
Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
....گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمانی ہوگی شَگُن پِہ دیکھو شادمانی ہوگی ...
....guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmānī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

def: s.m. A baby; a doll; a puppet; — a thick bough or branch; — a wind-gall.
more: n. m. 1. A thick bough or branch ( ṭahnā ). 2. A windgall. (?)
n. m. S. कुरुण्ड 1. A doll; puppet; an effigy. 2. A monument of infamy. guḍḍā banānā , v. a. 1. To make an effigy. 2. To set up as an object of scorn.
s. m. 1. A baby, doll. 2. A windgall. 3. Bough, branch.

def: s.f. The nape of the neck.
more: n. f. S. कन्धि neck. The nape of the neck. Aurat kī aql guddī pīchhe hotī hai . Prov. A woman's sense is in the back of her head. (She is wise when it is too late). guddī bhānnā , Slang, v. a. To slap one on the nape of his neck. guddī se zabān nikālnā , v. a. To extract one's tongue. (A punishment in the East).
n. f. 1. A paper kite. 2. See ḍhuḍḍī , 2.
s. f. Nape of the neck.

def: s.f. A paper kite; — a pinion; — an inferior kind of ḥuqqa.
more: s. f. 1. A paper kite. 2. A pinion. ¶ gaḍḍī, s. f. A bundle of paper containing ten quires.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To beat well.
adj. (f. -ī), 'Full of marrow or fat'; fat, plump; soft, downy, velvety (e.g. gudgudā bistar, 'a soft bed').
more: adj. Plump aud soft; downy; velvety. gudgudā bistar , n. m. A soft bed; velvet couch.

def: s.f. = gudgudī, q.v. s.f. Tickling, titillation; itching, prurience; prickly itching and burning (of a boil, &c.; or from heat in the system); — sexual desire
s. f. Titillation.
s.f. = gudgudī, q.v. s.f. Tickling, titillation; itching, prurience; prickly itching and burning (of a boil, &c.; or from heat in the system); — sexual desire

ety: fr. S. गुर्द्; or prob. fr. gudgud or gudgudā, q.v.
def: v.t. To tickle, titillate.
more: v. a. S. गुर्द्दन To tickle; titillate.
v. a. To tickle, to titillate.

def: s.f. Tickling, titillation; itching, prurience; prickly itching and burning (of a boil, &c.; or from heat in the system); — sexual desire
more: n. f. 1. Tickling; titillation. 2. Sexual desire.

def: adj. = H گدگلی गुदगुली gudgulī, s.f. (Dakh.) = gudgudā, and gudgudī, qq.v.
s.f. = H گدگلا गुदगुला gudgulā, adj. (Dakh.) = gudgudā, and gudgudī, qq.v.
v.t. = gūṅdhnā, q.v. v.n. To be kneaded; — to be plaited, &c.
s.f. = gūdṛī = gudṛī, q.v. s.f. A pallet, a beggar's bedding, quilt, &c.; a beggar's tattered garment, a quilt of rags and patches; a gaberdine.
more: s. f. See گودڙي gūdrī. ¶ godhṛī, s. f. See گودهڙا godhṛā.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = gūdā, q.v. s.m. The anus
s.f. (dialec.) A sheltered place for repairing boats, a small dock.
s.f. 1˚ = gudī; — 2˚ = gūdā, qq.v. s.f. (dialec.) = gūdā, q.v. s.m. The anus
s. (dakh.) Belabouring, kicking and cuffing. گديگم کرنا gudīgam karnā, To belabour.
adj. Desirous, wishful, wishing.
s.m. A soft thick blanket.
more: n. m. A soft thick blanket.

def: v.n. To be pricked, to be punctured; to be tattooed, &c. (see godnā)
s.m. (rare) = gudṛī, and gūdaṛ, qq.v. s.f. A pallet, a beggar's bedding, quilt, &c.; a beggar's tattered garment, a quilt of rags and patches; a gaberdine.

ety: gud, prob. = gūda, q.v.+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A pallet, a beggar's bedding, quilt, &c.; a beggar's tattered garment, a quilt of rags and patches; a gaberdine.
more: s. f. 1. A pallet, a beggarʼs bedding, quilts, &c. 2. A daily market.

def: s.f. = گدڙي gudṛī, q.v. s.f. A pallet, a beggar's bedding, quilt, &c.; a beggar's tattered garment, a quilt of rags and patches; a gaberdine.
more: s. f. 1. A quilt (especially, a faḳīrʼs ). 2. A bundle.

def: n. f. H. gūdaṛ . 1. A beggar's tattered garment or bedding; a ragged; quilt. 1. Pahnā libās khūb agar itr kā bharā, Yā chīthṛon kī gudṛī koī oṛh-kar marā . On death. Nazīr. Whether in robes that reek with perfume of the rose, Or in a ragged quilt the breath out of him goes? 2. Gar yār kī marzī huī sīr joṛ-ke baiṭhe, Ghar-bār chhuṛāyā to wahīṅ chhoṛ-ke baiṭhe, Moṛā jidhar us ne vahīṅ mūṅh moṛ-ke baiṭhe, Gudṛī jo silāī to use oṛh-ke baiṭhe, Aur shāl uṛhāyā to usī shāl meṅ khush haiṅ, Pūre haiṅ vohī mard jo har hāl meṅ khush haiṅ . Nazīr. If so my mistress willed I'd quiet sit, Or bade to leave my home by her I'd sit, Whichever way she turned me so I'd sit, Or made to sew a quilt, in it I'd sit, Or if wrapped in a shawl e'en so content, The perfect man is in each state content. 2. (Cor. of P. guzarī ) A daily market. 3. ( Slang ) Strength; power. Miyāṅ kī kyā gudṛī hai!
s.f. A daily market.
corr. of غدد g̠udad, or g̠udud, q.v.
s.m. A ball, &c. ( = gū or go, q.v.). s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.m. Forgiveness, pardon, remission of sins, absolution
more: (from غفر To cover, to forgive) s. m. Pardon, remission of sins. غفران پناه g̠ẖufrān-panāh, Asylum of pardon.

ety: v.n. of guftan = guptan; rt. old P. gub, fr. Zend khshub; S. क्षुभ्
def: s.f. Word, saying, speech, discourse (generally occurring in comp., e.g.)
subs: or guft-o-shunūd or shanīd, s.f. Conversation, discourse, dialogue, common talk, chitchat; altercation, dispute, debate, expostulation, controversy, contention squabble

def: part. pass. (of گفتن) Spoken, told.
part. Said, spoken, told, related.

ety: for guptan; rt. old P. gub; see guft
def: inf. & gerund, To say, speak, tell, relate; — saying, speaking, &c.
inf. or verb. noun, To say, saying.
adj. Fit to be said or spoken, &c.
more: adj. Fit to be told or spoken, capable of being told.

ety: guft, q.v.+tār = Zend tar; S. तार्
def: s.f. Speaking, saying, telling; speech, discourse, conversation; a saying; — a chapter.
more: n. f. گفتن to speak. Speech ( bāt , 3). Dastār aur guftār apnī hī kām ātī hai . Prov. Your turban your own hands must fold, your words must be your own.
(from گفتن) s. f. Speech, discourse, speaking, saying, telling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُفْتَار (guftār), from گفتن (“to say, tell”). By surface analysis, گُفْت (guft) + َار (ār).

def: s.f. See s.v. guft. s.f. Word, saying, speech, discourse (generally occurring in comp., e.g.)
subs: or guft-o-shunūd or shanīd, s.f. Conversation, discourse, dialogue, common talk, chitchat; altercation, dispute, debate, expostulation, controversy, contention squabble

def: n. f. Conversation; dialogue ( bāt , 3). guftgū k. , v. a. To converse. shushtah guftgū . Pure or polished language.
more: s. f. Conversation, discourse, dialogue, chitchat.

def: s. f. See گفتگو guft-gū.
s. f. Discourse, altercation, expostulation, debate, controversy, contention.
s. f. Discourse, altercation, expostulation, debate, controversy, contention.

s.m. The tree Amyris agallochum, or A. commiphora; the exudation of this tree, gum-resin; bdellium (of which there are many kinds; the name gūgal being applied locally to the fragrant gum-resin of the sāl, the Balsamodendron mukul, and several other trees).
more: n. m. S. गुग्गुलु Amyri's commiphorā , Wat.
s. m. 1. Name of a tree or gum. (Amyris agallocha: Roxb. Cor. Pl. Vol. 3.). 2. Bdellium.

def: s.m. The tree Amyris agallochum; a fragrant gum-resin (the exudation of the said tree), bdellium.
s.f.(?) Sulphur, brimstone; — gugird-ě-aḥmar, Red sulphur; — the philosopher's stone.
more: s. Sulphur. گوگرداحمر gogird-i-ahmar, Red sulphur; the philosopherʼs stone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گوگرد (gōgird, gūgird).

To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful

def: s.f. A cave, cavern; — secret place; hiding-place; the interior (of a thing); the heart.

def: s.f. Meconium (of infants).
s. f. Meconium (of infants).

ety: prob. corr. of gau-sāl, q.v.s.v. go
def: s.m. (dialec.) Cow-house, cow-shed.
s.f. A stye (in the eye).
more: s. f. A sty (in the eye).

def: s.m. = gauhar, q.v. s.m. Nature, essence, substance, stuff, matter; form; origin, root, stock, extraction; seed, offspring; — any hidden virtue; — intellect; wisdom; — a pearl; jewel, gem, precious stone
more: s. m. A gem, essence, &c. See گوهر.
subs: (of a gem, or a sword; — cf. jauhar)
adj. & s.m. Weighing or examining pearls; — a weigher or examiner of pearls (lit. or fig.); a critic
adj. Scattering gems; — elegant (discourse); s.m. An elegant discourse or composition.

def: s.f. Bawling, shouting; — commotion, tumult; alarm; — an unruly mob; — s.m. Helper, &c. ( = guhārī).
more: s. f. 1. An assistant, aid. 2. Bawling. 3. Tumult, alarm.

def: s.m. Helper, ally, assistant (i.q. gohrahā, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), One who is cried to for help; one who comes to the rescue; — an ally, assistant, helper, succourer.
adj. & s.m. = gauharī, q.v. adj. Innate, essential; — generous, noble; of good stock, high-bred; — s.m. A dealer in precious stones; a jeweller; a lapidary.
s.m. A place where dung or excrement (gūh) is thrown; a dung-hill.
more: s. m. A dunghill, a place where excrement is thrown.

def: part. adj. Covered, concealed.
more: part. Covered, concealed. Plaiting the hair.

ety: Prk. गुहंतं; S. गुफत्, rt. गुफ्
def: part. Threading; — plaiting (the hair, &c.).
s.m. A kind of venomous lizard (similar to the goh, q.v.).
n. m. A poisonous lizard.
more: adj. (dakh.) Deep, profound ( Harris ): see گنبهير gambhīr.

def: adj. Defiled with dung or excrement.
adj. = gūhī, q.v. adj. Defiled with dung or excrement.
s.m. A wood-louse.

ety: guh˚ = Prk. गुह(इ), or गुभ(इ) = S. गुफ(ति), rt. गुफ्
def: v.t. To thread, &c. ( = gūthnā, q.v.). v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

ety: guh˚ = Prk. गुह(इ) = S. गुफ (ति), rt. गुफ्
def: v.t. (Braj) = gūthnā, q.v. v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: v. a. (in Braj) 1. To thread, to string. 2. To plait, to braid.
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: v.t. & v.n. = gohrānā, q.v. v.n. & t. = gohārnā, q.v. v.n. (dialec.) To cry out, to call (to), to bawl, shout, halloo; — v.t. To call to, shout to, &c.
more: 1. v. n. To call out, to bawl. 2. v. a. To assist.

def: adj. To be covered or concealed, requiring concealment; concealable; private, secret; solitary, retired; — s.m. An organ of generation; the privities.
s.m. Name of a class of demigods who (like the Yakshas) are attendants of Kuvera, the god of wealth, and guardians of his treasures.

Wom. n. f. 1. A woman's female friend or playmate. Janam janam gun mānūṅgī, guiyāṅ, jo tū pī se milā. de, rī! Wom. Song. All my life I'll be grateful to you, dear, if you bring me and my love together. 2. A partner in a game.
s. m. A partner (at any game).

ety: prob. corr. of goyān; cf. guwaiyā
def: adj. & s.m. = goyāṅ, q.v.; — s.f. A (woman's) female companion or friend; — a partner (at any game).
s.f. = gojaʼī, q.v. s.m. A mixture of wheat and barley; — a crop of wheat and barley (or of barley and gram) sown together.
s.m. = gūjar, q.v.

s.m. (f. -ī, or -nī), Of or relating to Guzerāt; — an inferior caste of Rājpūts (chiefly engaged in agriculture, and notorious for cattle-stealing; they were originally from Guzerat, whence their name); a man of that caste; — a cowherd; a milkman.
more: n. m. gūjrī , n. f. S. गो चारक 1. A cowherd; a milkman. 2. An inferior Hin. caste chiefly engaged in agriculture, and notorious for cattle-stealing.
s. m. Name of an inferiour cast among Hindūs, so called, being originally from ( gurjara ) Gujrat.

def: s.f. corr. of guẕar, q.v. s.m. A passing; passage, transit; a ferry (cf. guẕāra, and guẕar).
s.m. Name of a province in Western India, Guzerat.
more: Name of a district of India, Guzarat.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi گُجْرات (gujrāt), derived from the name of the Gujjars.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Gujarāt; — a native of Gujarāt.

s.f. A woman of the Gūjar caste; the wife of a Gūjar ( = gūjarnī); a milkmaid; — name of a rāginī or musical mode; — an earthen image representing a milkmaid; — a heavy toothed anklet or wristlet of metal (worn by women of the Gūjar caste).
more: s. f. 1. Name of a rāgiṇī or musical mode. 2. The female of گوجر gūjar, q. v. 3. Name of an ornament worn on the wrists and feet. 4. An earthen image representing a milkmaid.

def: s.f. = gūjarnī, q.v.
s.f. A woman of the Gūjar caste; the wife of a Gūjar.
s.m. A kind of pastry (made of pounded almonds, pistachio nuts, &c.).
adj. Thick, &c. ( = gajhin, q.v.); — adv. Thickly, closely, densely. adj. Thick, close, dense, imporvious (as a forest, &c.).
s.f. A kind of ear-ring (syn. karṇ-phūl); — a kind of sweetmeat.
more: s. f. 1. An earring. 2. A kind of sweetmeat.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Hidden; secret, private; invisible (syn, gupt)
adj. S. गुह्य secret, Pr. gujjhao . Secret; invisible. gujjhī mār, yā choṭ . See gupt mār .
s.f. = gūjrī, q.v.
s.f. corr. of guẕrī, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Market, square of a city: see چوک chauk.

def: s.m. A frog; a toad; — a butt (for archers).
more: s. m. 1. A frog, a toad. 2. A butt for archers.

ety: old P. varedha; Armen. & Ar. ward; S. फूल
def: s.m. A rose; a flower; a red patch (on anything); — snuff (of a lamp or a candle); — albugo, speck or pearl (in the eye); — a seton, an issue; — a mark made (on the skin) by burning, a brand; a round spot of lime applied to the temple (as a remedy for inflammation of the eye); — a ball of prepared charcoal (used for burning the tobacco in a ḥuqqa); — the caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a ḥuqqa after smoking
subs: s.m. The flower Plumeria acuminata, or P. alba
s.m. Calendula; — the marigold, Linum trigynum (syn. gendā)
s.m. The sun-flower, Helianthus annuus (syn. sūraj-mukhī)
adj. Scattering roses; strewed with flowers
adj. 'With a body like a rose or flower'; delicate, slender; graceful; — of a pleasing nature
s.m. The globe amaranth, or bachelor's buttons, Gomphrena globosa
(-meṅ?), To char the end (of the match of a gun)
s.f. The note of the nightingale; warbling; — sound; — fame, rumour; — glad tidings; — a loud shout; a war cry, the cry of Allāh, Allāh, when rushing to battle
adj. 'With a body like the rose' (epithet of a mistress); delicate, graceful (syn. gul-andām); — s.f. A delicate, graceful, or lovely woman; — s.m. A kind of silk cloth
s.m. A rose-leaf; (met.) a mistress; — lip (of a mistress)

v.n. To flower, to blossom, to bud; — a strange or wonderful thing to happen or to come to light

s.m. The flower of the Mahuʼa or Bassia latifolia
To strew flowers; — to live in a bed of roses (or in clover); to live expensively; — to be addicted to pleasure

s.m. A fire-place (in a bath, &c.); a stove; a furnace; — gul-ḵẖan-tāb, s.m. One who lights the furnace (of a bath, &c.); — gul-ḵẖan-tābī, s.f. Heating or kindling of a stove or furnace
or gul-ě-ḵẖairū, or gul-ḵẖairū, s.m. The gilliflower (esp. the yellow kind); — Chinese and Persian hollyhock, Althea rosea
s.m. A handful of roses; a bunch of flowers; a nosegay

adj. Rosy-faced, rosy, ruddy; — s.f. A sweetheart, a mistress
adj. Scattering roses or flowers; — s.m. A kind of firework, matches with or without stars
s.m. A garden of roses; a bed of roses; a garden; — a flourishing and well-populated town; — adj. Blooming, flourishing
s.m. A rose-garden; a flower-garden; — name of a celebrated Persian book in prose and verse, by Shaikh Saʻdī Musliḥuʼd-dīn of Shīraz
s.m. Flower of the hermodactyl plant, Colchicum illyricum
s.f. A remarkably beautiful and odoriferous Persian (Syrian?) rose
s.f. Tuberose, Polianthes tuberosa (it is called 'shab-bo' from its giving forth scent in the night alone)
or gul-shakarī (in Persian, also gul-bā-shakar, or gul-ba-shakar), s.f. Sugar mixed with dried rose-leaves; conserve of roses

s.m. The hundred-leaved rose, Rosa centipetela; — the sun

or gul-ě-ʻajūba, s.m. The flower Hibiscus mutabilis

v.n. To be cauterized; to cauterize oneself (a practice among lovers, who burn themselves with heated pieces of coin, &c., as a proof of their love)
(-ko), To throw a squib at (a person); — to sow dissension, cause a disturbance; to poke fun at
or gul-kīs, s.m. The cock's-comb flower, Amaranthus cruentus

v.n. To be extinguished; to go out (a lamp or candle).

def: s.f. A channel for irrigation, a canal; a dyke, trench; — a path, road; — an unripe bunch of Indian corn.
more: n. m. A channel by which water is conveyed to a field ( barhā ).

def: s.m. The cheek; — a mouthful (used as first member of compounds, e.g. gal-phūlā, &c. q.v.s.v.).
more: n. m. 1. A flower. 2. A rose. 3. An ornament. Woh mahfil meṅ gul haiṅ . 4. (H. phūl ) The snuff of a candle. 5. The albugo, speck, or pearl in the eye; leucoma . 6. A round spot of lime applied to the temples in inflammation of the eyes. 7. A brand; seared flesh. 8. Balls of charcoal, used for burning the tobacco in a huqqā . 9. The caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a huqqa after smoking. gul-āchīn , n. m. A flower; Plumeria acuminata , Wat. gul-i-ashrafī ; H. basantī , The marigold; Linum trigynum , Roxb. gul-i-āftāb ; H. sūraj-mukhī , n. m. The sunflower; Helianthus annuus , Wat. gul-afshāṅ , Scattering roses; strewed with flowers. gul-anās kī jaṛ , n. f. Salep. (?) gul-i-auraṅg , n. Bachelor's buttons. gul bāṅdhnā , v. a. To char the end of the match of a gun. gul-badan , n. m. Striped silk cloth. gul-badan, gul-andām , adj. Poet. Fair as the rose (the body). gul-barg ; H. …

s. m. 1. A rose, a flower. 2. The snuff of a candle. 3. The albugo. 4. A seton, an issue, a mark made by burning, brand. 5. Balls of charcoal, used for burning the tobacco in a ḥuḳḳa. 6. The caput mortuum of tobacco left on the tile of a ḥuḳḳa after smoking. گلاچين gul-āchīn, s. m. The name of a flower. (Plumieria). گل اشرفي gul-i-ashrafī, Name of a flower. 1. Calendula: Bruce, M. S. 2. Linum trigynum: Roxb. گل افشان gul-afshān, Scattering roses, strewed with flowers. گل اناس کي جڙ gul-anās kī jaṛ, s. f. Salep. گل اندام gul-andām, adj. Slender, delicate, of a pleasing nature. گل اورنگ gul-i-aurang, s. m. Name of a flower. گل باندهنا gul bāndhnā, To char the end of the match of a gun. گلبرگ gul-barg, s. m. 1. A rose-leaf. 2. ( met. ) A mistress, lip (of a mistress). گل پياده gul-piyāda, s. m. A species of rose without smell. گل پيرهني gul-pairahan-ī, s. f. Delicacy of person. گل جعفري gul…
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.

def: s.m. A kind of goblin or demon; an imaginary sylvan demon of different shapes and colours (supposed to devour men and animals; from this the European loup-garou, or man-wolf, seems to be borrowed)
more: n. m. See (جماعت 1, 2, 5). 1. A crew; batch; throng. 2. A concourse; muster; mob; faction. 3. Crowds upon crowds.
n. m. An imaginary sylvan demon, supposed to devour men and animals.

(vulg. g̠ẖol ) s. m. 1. An imaginary sylvan demon of different shapes and colours, supposed to devour men and animals. (From this our European loup-garou, or man-wolf, seems to be borrowed). 2. (in Hindī) A corps, division, or main body of an army, a squadron, a crowd, gang. غول بياباني g̠ẖūl-i-bayābān-ī, A demon (inhabitant of the woods). غول کا غول g̠ẖol kā g̠ẖol, Crowd upon crowd.
subs: s.m. A goblin or demon that haunts woods or deserts.

ety: contrac. of غلغل g̠ulg̠ul; cf. g̠ulū
def: s.m. Noise, din, clamour, confusion of voices, outcry, tumult
subs: or g̠ul-gapāṛā, s.m. Clamour, shouts, disturbance, brawl, uproar, &c.
or g̠ul maćānā, v.n. To make a noise, raise a cry or an outcry, to bawl, shout, to make a tumult.

def: s.m. A ball; a bullet; cannon-ball ( = golā); — a cotton-reel; cotton-pod; — a ringlet, curl.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.m. Rose-water; (in India, also) a (red) rose; — adj. Rose-scented ( = gulābī)
more: n. m. 1. A rose. 2. ( arq-i-gulāb ) Rose-water. gulāb-pāsh , n. f. A bottle from which rosewater is sprinkled. gulāb-pāshī , n. f. Sprinkling rose-water. gulāb-pāshī k. , v. a. To sprinkle rose-water. gulāb-jāman , n. f. 1. The rose-apple; Eugenia jambos , Wat. 2. A sweetmeat. gulāb kī pattī , n. f. 1. Rose leaf. 2. ( Poet .) Thin lips. gulāb', gulābī , adj. Rose-scented. 1. Gulāb haiṅ gaṅḍeryāṅ pauṅḍe kī! Dehlī Street cry. 2. Gulābī peṛā hai qand kā ! Dehlī Street cry.

s. m. 1. Rosewater. 2. (Hind.) A rose. گلاب پاش gulāb-pāsh, s. f. A bottle from which rosewater is sprinkled. گلاب پاشي gulāb-pāsh-ī, s. f. Rosewater-sprinkling. گلاب پاشي کرنا gulāb pāshī karnā, To sprinkle with rosewater. گلاب چشم gulāb-chashm, adj. Meek-eyed. گلابنير gulāb-nīr, s. m. Rosewater.
subs: s.f. A bottle for sprinkling rose-water, a rose-water bottle
s.f. The sprinkling of rose-water; — gulāb-pāshī karnā, To sprinkle rose-water
s.f. The rose-apple, Eugenia jambos; — a kind of sweetmeat (which has the shape of the fruit jāman)

def: adj. Of rose-water; of roses; rose-coloured, rosy, roseate; rose-scented; — s.m. Rose-colour; — red (or ruby) wine; — s.f. A bottle, flagon (for holding rose-water, or wine, &c.); — a kind of sweetmeat (scented with rose-water; cf. gulābī rauṛī)
more: n. m. 1. Red wine; ruby wine. 2. A vial or bottle for holding wine. 3. A sweetmeat. gulāb'ī, gul-gūṅ , Rosy; roseate. gulābī āṅkh , n. f. The eyes red with liquor, much admired by Indian lovers. gulābī jāṛā , n. m. Mild weather. gulābī raṅgat , Rose color.
1. s. m. Rose-colour. 2. s. f. Bottle, flagon. 3. A kind of sweetmeat. گلابي جاڙا gulābī jāṛā, s. m. The spring season, whilst the sun is in the fish and the ram: see بسنت basant.
subs: s.f. An eye red with wine-drinking (much admired by Indian lovers)
s.m. The spring season; a mild winter
s.m. or gulābī raṅgat, s.f. Rose-colour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُلَابِی (gulābī). By surface analysis, گُلاب (gulāb, “rosewater, rose”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s. f. The rose-apple. (Eugenia jambos: Roxb. ).

ety: for golāʼī; gol+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Roundness, rotundity.
s. f. Roundness, rotundity.
s.m. The red powder thrown about by the Hindūs in the Holī festival (it is generally the meal of rice, or barley, or siṅghāṛā dyed red with baqam wood, &c.)
more: n. m. The red powder thrown on one another at the Holī. ☞ Barley, rice, or siṅghāṛā meal colored red. gulāl uṛānā, gulāl pheṅknā , v. a. To throw gulāl on one. gulāl-bāṛī , n. f. gulāl bāṛā , n. m. A royal place for recreation; a garden next to a palace. gulāl tulsī , n. f. Ocymum basilicum , Wat. gulāl malnā , v. n. To rub gulāl on one's face.
s. m. A farinaceous powder died red, which the Hindūs throw on each other in the holī. گلال چشم gulāl-chashm, adj. Reddish eye. گلال باڙي gulāl-bāṛī, s. f. A royal pavilion, a tent.
subs: or gulāl pheṅknā, To throw gulāl about, or upon one another
s.m., or gulāl-bāṛī, s.f. A royal pavilion (or place) for recreation; a garden adjoining a palace

def: adj. Of the colour of gulāl; stained or dyed with gulāl; — s.f. A kind of plant or weed.
more: adj. Of the colour of the gulāl or stained with it.

ety: v.n. fr. غلم 'to be or become excited by lust or appetence,' &c.
def: s.m. A boy, youth; a young man arrived at maturity; — a slave, bondsman (syn. banda); a servant (who receives food and clothing, but no wages); — your slave, your humble servant (see banda); — the knave (at cards); — a suit in ganjīfa
more: n. m. 1. A slave; bondsman; helot; serf. 2. Among Mahomedans, a household servant who receives food and clothing but no wages. 1. Gulām kī zāt se vafā nahiṅ . Prov. The race of slaves a faithless brood. 2. Jo bande khās haiṅ, voh banda-i-avām nahīṅ, Hazār bār jo Yūsaf bike gulām nahīṅ . Vazīr. Whoso is slave to One (God) to all a slave is not, Joseph though sold a thousand times a slave is not. 3. A youth concealed from his birth till the age of puberty. 4. Used like bandah , for the pronoun I or me by an inferior addressing a superior. 5. ( Cards ) The knave. 6. A suit in Ganjfah. gulām-i-zar-kharīd , A purchased slave. gulām kā tilām . A slave of a slave. gulām k., yā banānā , v. a. To enslave; reduce to slavery. Ek nigāh meṅ gulām karte haiṅ, Khūb-rū khūb kām karte hain! Walī. One glance shot from her eye, all slaves are made, Such pretty work by pretty faces made! gulām-gardish , n. …

(Heb. from To be concealed) s. m. 1. A boy or youth (from birth till puberty; q. d. concealed in his fatherʼs house and not yet appearing in publick: Buxt. ). 2. (from غلم g̠ẖalima, Coeundi cupidus fuit) A young man arrived at maturity. 3. (in Persian and Hindī) A slave. غلام پاره g̠ẖulām-pāra, Puerorum amoribus deditus. غلام گردش g̠ẖulām-gardish, s. m. A shed for servants to sit under. (Pers. plur. غلامان).
subs: or g̠ulām banānā, v.t. To make a slave of, to enslave
s.m. A shed for servants to sit under; a covered way (round a mosque, or building, &c.); the enclosed verandah of a tent
s.m. A blanket; any cheap thing of great use
or g̠ulāmoṅ-kī kār-o-bār, Sale or purchase of slaves, slave-dealing.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غُلَام (ġulām), from Arabic غُلَام (ḡulām). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi غلام (ġlam /⁠ġulām⁠/).

ety: g̠ulām, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Slavery, servitude, service
more: n. f. Slavery; bondage; thraldom; serfdom; vassalage; enslavement; involuntary servitude. gulāmī ikhtiyār k. , v. a. To serve one as a slave. gulāmī-khat . A certificate of slavery. gulāmī kā khat likhnā , To bind oneself to slavery by a bond. gulāmī meṅ denā , v. a. To give in marriage.

def: s. (dakh. Harris ) See گولنداز gol-andāz.

ety: for gullar = gular; S. गुड+र, or ल
def: s.f. The wild fig, Ficus glomerata (syn. ḍūmar); — a round pod (of cotton, &c.).
more: n. m. S. उदुम्बर 1. Wild fig; Ficus glomerata , Wat. /?/harne kā batāsā hī gūlar hai! Delhi Street cry. Wild figs from (the Kutab) waterfalls, ho! 2. Round pods, esp. of cotton. gūlar kā pet phaṛvānā , v. a. To get out a secret. Kyoṅ gūlar kā peṭ phaṛvā/?/ hai ? Prov. Saying. gūlar gapaknā . To be mealy-mouthed.
s. f. A wild fig. (Ficus guleria).

def: adj. Consisting of soft lumps (as boiled rice, khićaṛī, &c.); soft and lumpy; soft; — s.f. Rice and water boiled together and seasoned with salt (generally used in dysentery).
more: n. f. Rice and water boiled together and seasoned with salt, used generally in dysentery. Dahī gulatthī pechish kī davā. gulat'thī , adj. Soft like pap or pish-pash. Chāval gulatthī ho gaë .

def: = P گلبن gul-bun, See s.v. gul.
more: 1. s. m. A kind of silk-cloth. 2. adj. With a body like the rose (epithet of a mistress or of a beautiful person).

def: s.f. See s.v. gul. s.m. Noise, din, clamour, confusion of voices, outcry, tumult
more: s. f. 1. Warbling. 2. Fame, rumour. 3. Sound. 4. The war cry, the cry of Allāh Allāh when rushing to battle. 5. Glad tidings.
subs: or g̠ul-gapāṛā, s.m. Clamour, shouts, disturbance, brawl, uproar, &c.
or g̠ul maćānā, v.n. To make a noise, raise a cry or an outcry, to bawl, shout, to make a tumult.

def: = P گلبدن gul-badan, See s.v. gul.

def: s.m. A musketeer; rifleman; gunner; — a marksman (syn golī-ćalāne-wālā).
n. m. A slap on the cheek.
s. m. A musketeer, a gunner.
s. m. The flowers of mahūʼā (Bassia latifolia: Roxb. ).
s.f. = P گلخن gul-ḵẖan, s.m. See s.v. gul.
s.f. A pellet-bow
more: n. f. A pellet-bow. gulel-bāz, gulelchī , n. m. One who shoots with a pellet-bow. gulel chalānā, chhoṛnā, lagānā, yā mārnā , v. a. To shoot with a pellet-bow.
s. f. A pellet-bow. غليل باز g̠ẖulel-bāz, s. m. A shooter with a pellet-bow. غليل بازي g̠ẖulel-bāz-ī, s. f. Shooting with a pellet-bow. غليلچي g̠ẖulel-chī, A shooter with a pellet-bow.
subs: s.m. One who shoots with a pellet-bow
or ćhoṛnā, or lagānā, or mārnā, To shoot with a pellet-bow.

def: s.f. A pellet-bow.
more: (see غليل perhaps from Pers. گلوله or Sans. गोल A ball) s. f. A pellet-bow.

def: s.m. = غليله g̠ulela, q.v.
s.m. = g̠alūla or g̠ulla, q.v.
s.m. One who shoots with a pellet-bow ( = g̠ulel-bāz).
s.m. = गिलौंदा gilauṅdā, q.v. s.m. The flower (or the pod) of the mahuʼā or Bassia latifolia (cf. guleṅdā).
= P گلگون gul-gūṅ, See s.v. gul.
s.m. Noise, tumult, commotion, &c. ( = g̠ul, q.v.); — a kind of dish.
s.m. A sweet cake fried in ghī (wheat flower, sugar, and dahī, with anise and cardamom seeds, made into balls or dumplings and fried in ghī); — adj. (f. -ī), Soft; plump, sleek (syn. gudgudā).
more: n. m. S. गुड गोल Sweet cakes fried in oil or ghī. Kyā gul gulā sī nāk hai ! Chaff. Phabtī.
s. m. Sweet cakes fried in butter, or, wheat flower, sugar and dahī, with anise and cardamom seeds, made into dumplings and fried in ghī.

def: s.f. (dialec.) Swollen rice mixed with molasses formed into balls.
more: s. (dakh.) Swollen rice mixed with molasses formed into balls: see گلگلا gulgulā.

def: s.f. (dialec.) A jackdaw.
